questions.question: Explain what is meant by coordinate geometry.
questions.answer: Coordinate geometry is the branch of mathematics that uses algebra and coordinates to study geometric figures. It represents points on a plane using ordered pairs of numbers.

questions.question: Explain the terms abscissa and ordinate.
questions.answer: The abscissa is the x-coordinate of a point and represents the distance from the y-axis. The ordinate is the y-coordinate and represents the distance from the x-axis.

questions.question: What are the coordinates of a point lying on the x-axis and y-axis?
questions.answer: A point on the x-axis has coordinates of the form (x, 0) and a point on the y-axis has coordinates of the form (0, y).

questions.question: Define the origin of the coordinate plane.
questions.answer: The origin is the point where the x-axis and y-axis intersect. Its coordinates are (0, 0).

questions.question: Derive the distance formula between two points in a plane.
questions.answer: The distance formula is derived using the Pythagoras theorem. The distance between P(x₁, y₁) and Q(x₂, y₂) is √[(x₂ − x₁)² + (y₂ − y₁)²].

questions.question: Find the distance of a point from the origin.
questions.answer: The distance of a point P(x, y) from the origin O(0, 0) is √(x² + y²).

questions.question: Explain how the distance formula is useful in coordinate geometry.
questions.answer: The distance formula helps in finding lengths of line segments, verifying geometric shapes and checking collinearity of points.

questions.question: Explain what is meant by collinear points.
questions.answer: Collinear points are points that lie on the same straight line.

questions.question: Explain the condition for collinearity using distance formula.
questions.answer: Three points are collinear if the sum of the distances between two pairs of points equals the distance between the remaining pair.

questions.question: What is the section formula?
questions.answer: The section formula is used to find the coordinates of a point dividing a line segment joining two points in a given ratio internally.

questions.question: Explain internal division of a line segment.
questions.answer: Internal division means the dividing point lies between the two given points on the line segment.

questions.question: State the section formula for internal division.
questions.answer: The coordinates of a point dividing the line segment joining A(x₁, y₁) and B(x₂, y₂) internally in the ratio m₁ : m₂ are ((m₁x₂ + m₂x₁)/(m₁ + m₂), (m₁y₂ + m₂y₁)/(m₁ + m₂)).

questions.question: Explain the midpoint formula.
questions.answer: The midpoint formula gives the coordinates of the midpoint of a line segment joining two points. It is ((x₁ + x₂)/2, (y₁ + y₂)/2).

questions.question: Explain why midpoint divides a line segment in the ratio 1 : 1.
questions.answer: The midpoint lies exactly halfway between the two endpoints, dividing the line segment into two equal parts.

questions.question: Explain how distance formula helps in identifying a right-angled triangle.
questions.answer: By verifying whether the square of the largest side equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides using distances.

questions.question: Explain how coordinate geometry links algebra with geometry.
questions.answer: It uses algebraic equations to represent and solve geometric problems involving figures and distances.

questions.question: Explain the importance of the coordinate plane.
questions.answer: The coordinate plane helps in locating points precisely and studying geometric figures numerically.

questions.question: Explain how distance between two towns can be found using coordinates.
questions.answer: By representing the towns as points on a coordinate plane and applying the distance formula.

questions.question: Explain why distance is always a non-negative quantity.
questions.answer: Distance represents length and length cannot be negative.

questions.question: Explain the term ordered pair.
questions.answer: An ordered pair (x, y) represents the coordinates of a point in a plane where x is the abscissa and y is the ordinate.

questions.question: Explain the use of perpendicular axes in coordinate geometry.
questions.answer: Perpendicular axes help in locating points accurately on the coordinate plane.

questions.question: Explain the term locus in coordinate geometry.
questions.answer: A locus is the set of all points that satisfy a given condition.

questions.question: Explain how section formula is derived.
questions.answer: The section formula is derived using similar triangles and proportionality.

questions.question: Explain why section formula is important in real-life applications.
questions.answer: It helps in locating points that divide distances in given ratios such as towers, relay stations and landmarks.

questions.question: Summarise the key concepts of coordinate geometry.
questions.answer: Coordinate geometry deals with plotting points, finding distances, checking collinearity and dividing line segments using algebraic formulas.
